description: Shared workflow for either full issue resolution or planning-only Codex-reference design and implementation-plan handoff, with explicit review gates, an issue-resolution user-facing documentation refresh step, a final commit-message step, a command-node git commit/push step, and return paths.

prompts.divedraPromptTemplate: Coordinate {{workflowId}}. Run Step 1 intake first. If Step 1 classifies independent feature fanout items, dispatch feature-local design and implementation-plan work through the bounded fanout transition, join at Step 5 feature-plan join, then continue to Step 6 for issue-resolution mode or Step 9 for planning-only mode. If Step 1 does not classify fanout items, run the sequential path: Step 2 design-doc update, Step 3 design review, Step 4 implementation-plan creation, and Step 5 implementation-plan review. If Step 3 reports any high or mid findings, route back to Step 2. If Step 5 reports design changes are required, route back to Step 2. If Step 5 reports plan-only changes are required, route back to Step 4. After Step 5 acceptance for a planning-only run, run Step 9 commit-message creation, then Step 10 command-node commit and push, then finish. Otherwise run Step 6 implementation, then Step 7 implementation review. If Step 7 reports any high or mid findings, route back to Step 6. After Step 7 acceptance, run Step 8 user-facing documentation refresh, then Step 9 commit-message creation, then Step 10 command-node commit and push, then finish. Finish only when the required review gates are accepted and the final workflow changes have been committed and pushed for the selected mode.
prompts.workerSystemPromptTemplate: Return concise business JSON only. Keep workflow mode, issue references, codex-agent references, file paths, findings, review decisions, and verification commands explicit.

steps.description: Start the shared workflow and hand execution to the intake worker.
Normalize the request into a shared intake brief for either issue resolution or planning-only reference work.
Update the design documents from the accepted intake brief before planning or implementation.
Review the design update and return to Step 2 when any high or mid finding exists.
Create or revise the implementation plan from the accepted design.
Review the implementation plan and final design-plan consistency, then return to the correct authoring step when blocking findings exist.
Join feature-local design and implementation-plan branch outputs before planning-only completion or dependency-aware implementation.
Implement the accepted plan, run verification, and update plan progress.
Review the implementation diff and return to Step 6 when any high or mid finding exists.
Refresh the user-facing repository documentation after implementation acceptance and before commit generation.
Emit the final change summary and commit message after planning-only acceptance or implementation acceptance.
Use the Step 9 commit message in a command node that stages, commits, and pushes the accepted workflow changes.
Publish the accepted planning handoff or the final accepted issue-resolution summary.
